title: Do-bridge: promote a task to real work (coding -> bober run)
description: Promote a hub Finding/task into real work. Defines the promotesTo semantics and a promotion mechanism that, after passing the existing approve gate, launches the coding/project proof path (Finding -> detached `agent-bober run` via runPipeline) through an extensible registry of domain/kind promoters, then records the run link and lifecycle outcome back on the Finding.

features.title: Promoter registry + FindingStore port + promotesTo semantics
features.description: An extensible registry mapping a PromoterKey {domain, kind?} to a Promoter, a narrow FindingStore port (with a FactStore-backed adapter and an in-memory fake) so the do-bridge is testable in isolation from the sibling task-inbox/priority-hub storage, and the concrete PromotionRef shape that gives Finding.promotesTo its meaning.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: PromoterRegistry.resolve(finding) returns the most specific match (domain+kind, then domain-only) and undefined when no promoter is registered for the finding's domain.
AC2: A coding promoter converts a domain='coding'|'projects' Finding into a PromotionPlan {kind:'bober-run', task, teamId?}.
AC3: `bober do <findingId> --dry-run` prints the planned launch and mutates nothing (no Finding write, no approval marker, no spawn).

features.title: Approve-gated launch of real work
features.description: `bober do <findingId>` writes a pending approval marker summarizing the plan, gates on it through the existing .bober/approvals marker mechanism (interactive prompts confirm in a TTY, external `bober approve`/`bober reject` in non-TTY, `--yes` auto-approve), and only on approval launches the coding work through an injected Launcher port whose default adapter wraps the chat RunSpawner.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: On approval the Launcher is invoked exactly once with the promoter's task and the Finding gains promotesTo {runId, status:'launched'} with status open->in-progress.
AC2: On rejection the Launcher is never invoked, the pending marker is removed, and the Finding remains at status 'open'.
AC3: The promotion reuses src/state/approval-state.ts markers so `bober approve promote-<id>` resolves it; no new approval format is introduced.

features.title: Outcome reconciliation + registry extensibility
features.description: reconcilePromotions reads the linked run's run-state.json and advances the Finding (completed->done via supersede, aborted/failed->open, running->unchanged), exposed via `bober do --reconcile` and run best-effort at the start of every `bober do`. A second registered stub promoter and docs/do-bridge.md prove and document the extension point for future medical/financial promoters.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: A 'completed' run-state transitions the linked Finding in-progress->done and sets promotesTo.status='completed'; 'aborted' returns it to 'open'; 'running' leaves it unchanged.
AC2: Registering a second promoter under a different PromoterKey resolves for that domain/kind while an unregistered pair fails closed with a clear error.
AC3: docs/do-bridge.md documents the PromoterRegistry.register extension point and the Promoter interface.

assumptions: task-inbox (spec-20260628-task-inbox) persists Findings on the shipped FactStore (src/state/facts.ts:136). Evidence: research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md §3a line 128 'Rides on FactStore machinery already shipped'. The do-bridge reads/writes Findings through a FindingStore port; the default adapter is FactStore-backed and an in-memory fake isolates do-bridge tests from the exact encoding.

priority-hub (spec-20260628-priority-hub) adds the optional `promotesTo?` field to the Finding schema (research §3a line 126). The do-bridge defines its concrete PromotionRef shape {kind:'bober-run', runId, launchedAt, status:'launched'|'completed'|'aborted'} and the helpers that read/write it.
Coding/project Findings carry domain in {coding, projects} (research §3a line 132 'coding task -> bober run'); the coding promoter is keyed on those domains.

The work-launcher reuses the chat RunSpawner (src/chat/run-spawner.ts:97), which shells `agent-bober run <task> --run-id <id>` detached (run-spawner.ts:113) -> runRunCommand (src/cli/commands/run.ts:181) -> runPipeline (src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:970). Launching detached (not in-process) avoids blocking the CLI for the duration of a multi-hour run.

The approve gate reuses the disk markers in src/state/approval-state.ts (savePending:49, saveApproved:106, saveRejected:122, deletePending:138) -> the same gate `bober approve`/`bober reject` (src/cli/commands/approve.ts) and the chat inline buttons resolve; non-TTY waiting mirrors the poll loop in src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/disk.ts.

Run terminal state is readable via src/state/run-state.ts readRunState; RunSpawner writes status running->aborted there (run-spawner.ts:160-167) and the pipeline writes completed. Marking a Finding done = supersede (src/state/facts.ts:291) per research §3a line 131 'Completion = supersede'.
outOfScope: Task capture and lifecycle commands (bober task add|list|done|snooze|drop) - owned by spec-20260628-task-inbox.
Cross-repo finding aggregation, the ranking lens-panel, and the Finding schema definition - owned by spec-20260628-priority-hub.
Medical and financial domain promoters - future specs; only the extension point is delivered here.
Calendar planner, Telegram frontend, and scheduler (research §3a/§3.2).
A fleet-backed Launcher implementation - the Launcher port is kept open for it but only the RunSpawner-backed adapter is built.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the work-launcher run the pipeline in-process (runPipeline await) or spawn a detached `agent-bober run` child?

options.label: A
options.description: In-process runPipeline await - simplest, but blocks the CLI for the whole multi-hour run.

options.label: B
options.description: Detached spawn via the chat RunSpawner - returns immediately with a runId to link and reconcile later.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B - the chat layer already reuses the run entry this way (src/chat/run-spawner.ts:97); a detached run yields a linkable runId without freezing the CLI.

clarificationQuestions.question: Should the approve gate use a brand-new confirmation file format or reuse the existing .bober/approvals markers?

options.label: B
options.description: Reuse src/state/approval-state.ts savePending/saveApproved/saveRejected so `bober approve`/`bober reject` and chat buttons already work.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B - reusing the shipped gate (approval-state.ts:49) keeps one approval surface across run, chat, and do-bridge.

clarificationQuestions.question: How is a promoted Finding marked 'done' given the launched run is detached?

options.label: A
options.description: Block and poll the run to completion inside `bober do`.

options.label: B
options.description: Snapshot reconciliation: a reconcilePromotions step reads run-state.json on demand and supersedes the Finding when the run reached a terminal state.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: B - reads readRunState (run-spawner.ts:160-167) and supersedes (facts.ts:291) without blocking; matches research §3a 'Completion = supersede'.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Autonomous self-approval. The locked research design (research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md §3a, esp. lines 126-132) plus the concrete shipped substrate (runPipeline, RunSpawner, approval-state markers, FactStore) fully determine the do-bridge shape; no high-stakes question remained unresolved, so the plan proceeds without user clarification.
B - detached spawn via the chat RunSpawner (src/chat/run-spawner.ts:97), which shells `agent-bober run` -> runPipeline; returns a linkable runId without blocking the CLI.
B - reuse the existing .bober/approvals markers (src/state/approval-state.ts:49) so `bober approve`/`bober reject` and chat inline buttons resolve promotions with no new format.
B - snapshot reconciliation via reconcilePromotions reading run-state.json (run-spawner.ts:160-167) and superseding the Finding (facts.ts:291); no blocking poll.

techNotes.dataModel: Finding (schema owned by priority-hub) gains a concrete promotesTo: PromotionRef { kind:'bober-run', runId, launchedAt, status:'launched'|'completed'|'aborted' }. PromoterRegistry maps PromoterKey { domain:string, kind?:FindingKind } -> Promoter { canPromote(finding), plan(finding): PromotionPlan }. PromotionPlan = { kind:'bober-run', task:string, teamId?:string }.
securityConsiderations: No work is launched without an explicit approve resolution (TTY confirm, `bober approve`, or `--yes`).
The Launcher is an injected port so unit tests never spawn a real process.
reconcilePromotions and start-of-command reconcile are best-effort and never abort the CLI.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: A promotion never launches work without an explicit approval resolution.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test: the reject path and the no-resolution path invoke the Launcher zero times.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Adding a new domain promoter requires only a PromoterRegistry.register() call plus a Promoter implementation - no edits to the CLI handler or Launcher.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: manual: docs/do-bridge.md plus the Sprint 3 second-promoter test.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: reconcilePromotions and the start-of-command reconcile never abort the CLI on a missing or corrupt run-state file.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test: a missing run-state returns without throwing.

constraints: Additive only: do not modify the task-inbox or priority-hub modules; consume them through the FindingStore port and the Finding schema.
ESM with .js import extensions; TypeScript strict mode; no SDK imports outside providers/.
CLI handlers never throw - set process.exitCode and return (mirrors src/cli/commands/memory.ts).
Filesystem state only - approvals as .bober/approvals markers, findings/runs via FactStore + run-state (no new database).

metadata.dependsOnNote: task-inbox provides Finding capture/storage/lifecycle; priority-hub defines the Finding schema including the promotesTo field this spec gives meaning to. Both are upstream.
